title: Novae Linguae Eval Attestation (v0.1)
description: A signed eval attestation: a certifier's statement of MEASURED capability for a weights record (spec/weights.md rung 3) — the weights analogue of a function certification. Produced by `nl-validator attest-weights --sign <seed>`. Hashed and signed by exactly the same canonical-serialization rules as a Nova Locutio message (remove 'hash' and 'signature' before hashing; remove only 'signature' before signing; resolve the signer's key from the 'from' DID), differing only in the address prefix: `evl_<64 hex>`. The attestation graph ingests a signature-valid one as an `attests-eval` edge, so a consumer's local policy can answer 'is this model's measured score attested by a certifier I trust?' — for opaque bytes, this layer is the artifact's value.

kind.description: Discriminates this artifact as an eval attestation.

subject.description: Content-address of the weights record this attestation is about.

eval.description: What was measured: the harness, its settings, and the content identity of the task set — enough for an independent certifier to run the same eval.

settings.description: Harness settings the score depends on (e.g. conventions off, shots 0, dtype).

task_set.description: Content identity of the graded task set (e.g. the curated corpus sha256 + task count). The score is meaningless without it.

results.description: The measured scores. Convention: per-kind objects {pass, total} (surface-exact) with optional semantic counts, plus a 'total' entry — but any shape the named harness emits is admissible; the eval descriptor is what makes it interpretable.

timestamp.description: Optional ISO-8601 time the eval was run. Omitted for a byte-reproducible attestation.

from.description: DID of the attesting agent (the Ed25519 public key that signed this attestation).

hash.description: BLAKE3-256 of the canonical serialization of this attestation with 'hash' and 'signature' removed.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical serialization with only 'signature' removed (so the signature covers the hash).
